title: Smoky Shaggy Canadian Goose Hash
description: A smoky, shaggy Canadian dish featuring goose fat, perfect for a hearty breakfast or brunch, with a focus on raw-friendly and reduced ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Peel and dice the potatoes into 1-inch cubes.
In a large bowl, toss the diced potatoes with 2 tablespoons of goose fat, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the potatoes out in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes.
While the potatoes are roasting, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of goose fat in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Add the diced bell pepper to the skillet and cook until it is tender.
Once the potatoes are done roasting, add them to the skillet with the onion and bell pepper mixture.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Serve the smoky shaggy Canadian goose hash hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
